en:
When I first arrived in London, I went to a railway station alone.
I asked a porter if there was a cheap hotel nearby.
He answered quickly in a strong accent, and I understood nothing.
I repeated my question several times, but his reply was still unclear.
Neither of us could understand the other, though both of us spoke English.
At last I wondered whether the English really spoke English.
cn:
我第一次到伦敦时，一个人去了火车站。
我问一位搬运工附近是否有便宜旅馆。
他用很重的口音飞快回答，我一句也没听懂。
我把问题重复了好几次，他的答复仍然不清楚。
我们两人都说英语，却谁也听不懂谁。
最后我不禁怀疑英国人到底讲不讲英语。

en:
I study art and I think children are often the best critics.
My little sister always tells me honestly whether she likes my pictures.
She does not pretend that she understands difficult modern art.
Yesterday she looked at one of my paintings upside down and laughed.
Then she said that the pattern looked better that way.
Since then I have believed that simple eyes can appreciate true beauty.
cn:
我学习美术，而且我觉得孩子常是最好的评论家。
我妹妹总会诚实地告诉我她是否喜欢我的画。
她不会假装自己懂那些难懂的现代艺术。
昨天她把我一幅画倒过来看，然后笑了。
接着她说那样看图案反而更好。
从那以后我相信，简单的眼光也能欣赏真正的美。

en:
A group of boys camped in a field near a stream.
As soon as they arrived, they put up a tent and lit a fire.
After they cooked supper, they sat by the campfire and sang.
In the middle of the night heavy rain began and the stream rose.
Cold water crept into the tent while they were sleeping in their bags.
They woke up, packed quickly, and left the field in the dark.
cn:
一群男孩在小河边的田野里露营。
他们一到就搭起帐篷并生火。
做完晚饭后，他们围着篝火坐着唱歌。
半夜下起大雨，小河水位上涨。
他们睡在睡袋里时，冰冷的水慢慢渗进了帐篷。
他们醒来后迅速收拾，在黑暗中离开了田野。

en:
Jasper is one of the few men who still believe in ancient myths.
Drivers who parked outside his gate gave him endless trouble.
He put up signs, but they had no effect at all.
Then he hung a frightening head of Medusa in his garden.
Since he has done this, no one has parked there.
The face that turned men to stone has finally protected his gate.
cn:
贾斯珀是少数仍相信古老神话的人之一。
把车停在他门外的司机一直给他制造麻烦。
他竖过警示牌，但完全没有效果。
后来他在花园里挂了一个可怕的美杜莎头像。
自从他这样做后，再也没人把车停在那里。
那个能把人变成石头的脸，终于保护了他的大门。

en:
Captain Ben has bought a small plane and turned it into an air taxi.
He has carried passengers to lonely farms and remote islands.
He has accepted many unusual requests in recent years.
Once he flew to a block of flats and landed on the roof.
People below stopped and stared because they had never seen such a sight.
That single trip made his strange service famous.
cn:
本机长买了一架小飞机，把它改成了空中出租车。
他载过乘客去偏僻农场和遥远小岛。
这些年他接过许多不同寻常的请求。
有一次他飞到一栋公寓楼并降落在楼顶。
楼下的人都停下脚步，因为从没见过这种景象。
那一次飞行让他的奇特服务出了名。

en:
I often sit by the river and watch boats pass my garden.
One evening a man in a boat was moving slowly along the bank.
Suddenly a ball flew over the wall and hit him on the head.
He looked towards me angrily because he thought I had kicked it.
Without a word, he kicked the ball back into the water.
Then his boat moved past the bend and out of sight.
cn:
我常坐在河边看小船从花园前经过。
一天傍晚，一名男子坐在船里沿岸慢慢前行。
突然一个球飞过墙头，砸在他头上。
他愤怒地朝我看，因为他以为球是我踢的。
他一声不吭地把球一脚踢回河里。
随后小船驶过河湾，消失在视线中。

en:
Frank is a successful businessman who retired recently.
When he was young, he worked in a garage and earned very little.
By the time he was thirty, he had saved some money.
He had borrowed a little more before he opened his own workshop.
Years later his business grew into a large company.
Now his son manages it, and Frank tells this story with pride.
cn:
弗兰克是一位最近退休的成功商人。
他年轻时在修车行工作，收入很少。
到三十岁时，他已经存下一些钱。
开自己的作坊前，他又借了一点钱。
多年后，他的生意发展成了一家大公司。
现在公司由他儿子管理，而他总自豪地讲这段故事。

en:
A store detective was watching a woman in a large shop.
She was walking from counter to counter while assistants were busy.
She picked up small articles and slipped them into her coat.
The detective followed her quietly and waited for the right moment.
When she paid for one cheap item, he stopped her at the door.
She had tried to steal many things, but quick work caught her.
cn:
一名商店侦探正在大商场里盯着一位女人。
店员忙碌时，她正在各个柜台之间走来走去。
她拿起小物件，悄悄塞进自己的外套。
侦探安静地跟着她，等待合适时机。
当她只为一件便宜东西付款时，他在门口拦住了她。
她本想偷很多东西，但迅速行动让她落网了。

en:
A woman was sailing at night when a storm struck her boat.
The boat had hit rocks and had sunk before help could arrive.
In the darkness she saw a faint light along the distant coast.
She swam towards it for hours through cold water.
By the time dawn came, she had swum several miles.
Exhausted but alive, she finally reached the shore.
cn:
一位女子夜间航行时，风暴袭击了她的小船。
救援到来前，船已经撞礁并沉没。
在黑暗中，她看见远处海岸上一点微光。
她在冰冷海水中朝那点光游了好几个小时。
等到天亮时，她已经游了好几英里。
她精疲力竭但还活着，最终到达了岸边。

en:
Dan received a letter from the police and felt worried all morning.
He could not imagine why they wanted him at the station.
As soon as he arrived in the afternoon, a policeman greeted him kindly.
The officer told him that his bicycle had been found.
Dan was surprised because he had lost it ten years earlier.
The policeman smiled, and Dan thought it was quick work indeed.
cn:
丹收到警察来信后，整个上午都很担心。
他想不出他们为什么要他去警局。
下午他一到，一位警察就友好地接待了他。
警官告诉他，他的自行车找到了。
丹很吃惊，因为他十年前就把车丢了。
警察笑了笑，丹心想这办案速度可真“快”。

en:
Roy used to drive a taxi, but now he drives a bus.
Yesterday he was driving along a busy street when two thieves ran out of a bank.
They snatched a woman's bag and rushed across the pavement.
Roy drove straight at them and used his brakes at the last moment.
The thieves dropped the bag and one was caught by the crowd.
Roy became a local hero because he acted so bravely.
cn:
罗伊过去开出租车，现在开公共汽车。
昨天他在繁忙街道上行驶时，两名劫匪从银行冲了出来。
他们抢走一位女士的包，朝人行道飞奔。
罗伊开车直冲他们，并在最后一刻踩下刹车。
劫匪丢下包，其中一人被人群抓住。
罗伊因此成了当地英雄，因为他行动非常勇敢。

en:
Debbie is going to swim across the Channel tomorrow.
She has trained hard for years for this single attempt.
She will set out at five in the morning from the French coast.
At this time tomorrow she will be swimming in cold water.
Her parents will be waiting anxiously while millions will be watching on TV.
If the weather is kind, she will break the record.
cn:
黛比明天将要横渡英吉利海峡。
为了这次尝试，她多年来一直刻苦训练。
她将于清晨五点从法国海岸出发。
明天这个时候，她将正在冰冷海水中游泳。
她父母会焦急等待，同时数百万人将通过电视观看。
如果天气给力，她将打破纪录。
